summary: DEVAM CRM iteration 2 — tested all 5 new features (edit-in-place, lead drawer + activity timeline, inventory bulk-generator, RBAC users CRUD, public capture page) + regression. Backend: 16/16 pytest tests passed. Frontend: All flows passed after fixing 1 critical missing-useState bug in Projects.jsx / Builders.jsx (broke edit-in-place entirely).

ui_bugs.issue: ReferenceError: 'editing' is not defined — both pages used `editing`/`setEditing` but never declared the state. Pages were completely broken (white-screen runtime error). FIXED by testing agent: added `const [editing, setEditing] = useState(null);` in both files.

action_items: Review the missing-useState fix applied by tester in Projects.jsx and Builders.jsx — verify code-style consistency (same pattern as Leads.jsx). No further action required for functionality.
Optional: consider seeding role-based test users (e.g. one sales_agent) so admins can validate RBAC without manual invite.
critical_code_review_comments: Projects.jsx and Builders.jsx were missing `const [editing, setEditing] = useState(null)` — a regression caught only at render time. Adding an ESLint rule (no-undef) or pre-commit lint in /app/frontend would have caught this. Tester fixed both files.

PATCH endpoints for builders/projects/site-visits/inventory accept raw `dict` payload — bypasses Pydantic validation. Recommend defining BuilderUpdate/ProjectUpdate Pydantic models with `extra='ignore'` for type safety, similar to LeadUpdate.
/api/public/leads is public and rate-limit-free — could be spammed. Recommend adding basic IP-based rate-limiting (e.g. slowapi) before production.
Seed_data creates demo data only if `db.builders.count_documents({}) == 0` — that ignores partial seed states. Consider per-collection idempotent seed checks.
server.py is now 929 lines and growing — recommend splitting into modular routers (auth.py, leads.py, projects.py, users.py) before next iteration.

success_rate.frontend: 100% after fix (login, leads drawer, activity add, lead edit, inventory bulk modal, users CRUD + RBAC, project edit/share, builder edit, public capture form + thank-you state)

seed_data_creation: Backend tests left a few TEST_ prefixed leads/projects/users in DB. They use TEST_ prefix per guidelines. Not critical for production.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 2 complete. All 5 features (edit-in-place, lead activity drawer, bulk inventory, users RBAC, public capture page) verified end-to-end. Found & fixed missing useState bug in Projects/Builders. Public capture page accessible at /capture/{project_id}. Use http://<frontend>/capture/<project_id> to test. For new test users, use admin to invite via /app/users.
rca of the issue: Issue: /app/projects and /app/builders pages threw 'ReferenceError: editing is not defined' as soon as page rendered, breaking the entire iteration-2 edit-in-place feature in the UI. Reproduction: login → navigate to /app/projects → React error overlay appears with 'editing is not defined'. RCA: Projects.jsx & Builders.jsx referenced `editing`/`setEditing` in 4 places each (Edit button, Add-new button, Form open prop, Form close handler) without declaring the state hook. Mitigation: Added `const [editing, setEditing] = useState(null);` near the other useState calls in both files. Verified by reloading both pages — edit buttons render, clicking opens prefilled form modal, project-share button works.
